title: Sphaerostephanos neotoppingii
taxonomy: Sphaerostephanos neotoppingii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Sphaerostephanos, which comprises approximately 240 species of ferns. The genus is part of the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns known as the wood ferns. Sphaerostephanos neotoppingii is classified within the subclass Polypodiidae, which is a subgroup of the class Polypodiopsida. This fern classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other similar species.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues.
